description: OP_LSHIFT must truncate the shifted result to the original byte length (discarding overflow MSBs), matching BSV node behaviour. ts-sdk v2.0.5 threw 'byte array longer than desired length' because BigNumber.toArray asserted the result fit without first masking it to the input width.

regression.symptom: Spend.validate() threw 'byte array longer than desired length' for any OP_LSHIFT where the shifted value exceeded the input byte length
regression.root_cause: Spend.ts passed shiftedBn directly to toArray(length) which asserted result fits; the fix masks to original bit-width (buf1.length * 8 bits) before conversion

vectors.description: LSHIFT(0x6A09E667, 30) — 4-byte value shifted left 30 bits must truncate to 4 bytes
vectors.notes: 0x6A09E667 << 30 = 0x1A827999C0000000 (8 bytes of precision); truncated to low 32 bits = 0xC0000000. Real BSV nodes execute this correctly. The bug only affected the SDK off-chain interpreter.

vectors.description: LSHIFT(0x01, 7) — 1-byte operand, shift fits cleanly in 1 byte
vectors.notes: 0x01 << 7 = 0x80. No truncation needed — verifies the basic non-overflow case still works after the fix.

vectors.description: LSHIFT(0xFF, 1) — 1-byte operand, result overflows into 9 bits but must truncate to 1 byte
vectors.notes: 0xFF << 1 = 0x1FE; truncated to 8 bits = 0xFE. Simplest possible overflow case — one extra bit dropped.
